root_text.an5.147:1.3: Asakkaccaṁ deti, acittīkatvā deti, asahatthā deti, apaviddhaṁ deti, anāgamanadiṭṭhiko deti.
root_text.an5.147:2.3: Sakkaccaṁ deti, cittīkatvā deti, sahatthā deti, anapaviddhaṁ deti, āgamanadiṭṭhiko deti.

translation_text.an5.147:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five gifts of an untrue person.
translation_text.an5.147:1.3: They give carelessly. They give thoughtlessly. They don’t give with their own hand. They give the dregs. They give without consideration for consequences.
translation_text.an5.147:1.4: These are the five gifts of an untrue person.
translation_text.an5.147:2.1: There are these five gifts of a true person.
translation_text.an5.147:2.3: They give carefully. They give thoughtfully. They give with their own hand. They don’t give the dregs. They give with consideration for consequences.
translation_text.an5.147:2.4: These are the five gifts of a true person.”

variant_text.an5.147:1.3: acittīkatvā → acitiṁ katvā (sya-all); acittikatvā (pts1ed); acittiṁ katvā (mr) | apaviddhaṁ → apaviṭṭhaṁ (sya-all, km)
